a:r.a:t: The memory system handles extraction and storage well, but three enterprise gaps remain. First, conflict resolution -- when a user corrects a fact, both old and corrected versions persist and the agent sees contradictions. The system needs intelligent merging with timestamp-aware supersession. Second, scope isolation is too rigid -- user scope and conversation scope are hard walls with no pattern synthesis across sessions. User behavior, escalation history, and preferences cannot be tracked longitudinally. The planned cross_session_synthesizer would run periodic jobs to promote high-confidence user facts with confidence scores. Third, and most critical for regulated industries: compliance. Memory is currently write-once with no TTL enforcement, no PII redaction, and no right-to-forget capability. GDPR and CCPA require delete-on-request with auditable proof of erasure. The planned retention_policy.py would add per-scope TTLs, a Presidio-style PII classifier, and compliant deletion records written to the audit log before erasure. Memory v2 in persistence/postgres/memory_v2.py has laid the groundwork with MemoryEpisode types, importance scoring, TTL hints, and supersession tracking -- but enforcement of these policies is not yet active.
